title: Source allow-list schema + licence-gate policy (open-lead, restricted-stub rule)
context: Before any vocabulary or crosswalk work can begin, the project needs a machine-readable allow-list schema and a formal licence-gate policy that enforces the open-lead / restricted-stub rule. The binding Elyos cancer guardrails prohibit embedding or redistributing restricted terminologies (SNOMED CT, ICD-O-3, LOINC, ICCR). All sources must be individually approved by a licence reviewer before use. No sources are currently approved and no licence reviewer is engaged yet. See PLAN.md.
objective: Define the `sources/allowlist.yml` schema and the licence-gate policy document that: (1) describes required allowlist fields per source, (2) codifies the open-lead rule (Mondo/ORDO/HPO/OncoTree/NCIt/mCODE/OMOP/Phenopackets/DUO as primary bindings) and the restricted-stub rule (SNOMED CT/ICD-O-3/LOINC/ICCR = code-only crosswalk stubs, never embedded content), and (3) states the 'no artifact more permissive than its most-restrictive embedded source' licence inheritance rule.
acceptanceCriteria: `sources/allowlist.yml` schema defines required fields: name, custodian, URL, version, licence/legal basis, rights analysis, restricted (true|false), and status (approved|rejected|pending).
Policy states the open-lead rule: Mondo/ORDO/HPO/OncoTree/NCIt/mCODE/OMOP CDM/Phenopackets/DUO are the primary binding targets.

Policy states the restricted-stub rule: SNOMED CT, ICD-O-3, LOINC, and ICCR are categorically restricted; embedding proprietary descriptions or hierarchy is rejected; only code/identifier crosswalk stubs are permitted.
Policy states the 'no artifact more permissive than its most-restrictive embedded source' licence inheritance rule.
Policy requires that no source is used in any task until its allowlist entry has status: approved signed off by the licence reviewer.
Committed as a human-readable governance document plus the allowlist YAML schema via PR, with provenance to the Elyos cancer guardrails.
output: A `sources/allowlist.yml` schema file and a `governance/licence-gate-policy.md` policy document defining the allow-list schema, open-lead rule, restricted-stub rule, and licence inheritance rule, committed via PR.
